column1: # /Worksheet Delete Sheet — drop the current sheet.
# /WDS removes the worksheet at the pointer; sheets after it shift back
# one slot.  The pointer stays at the same column/row, on whatever
# sheet now sits at that index (clamped to the last remaining sheet).
# Deleting the only remaining sheet is refused — there must always be
# Seed sheet A and add a second sheet (B) with distinguishing data.
# /WIS After: new blank sheet appears as B; pointer stays on A.
# Hop to B and seed it.
# Hop back to A; pointer is now on the sheet we are about to delete.
# /WDS on sheet A: it is destroyed; B's data shifts back into slot A.
# Only one sheet remains; /WDS now is a no-op (last sheet protected).
